faqs.question: When does Medicare open enrollment start in Alabama?
faqs.answer: Alabama follows the federal calendar: Annual Enrollment runs October 15 through December 7, the Medicare Advantage Open Enrollment Period runs January 1 through March 31, and your Initial Enrollment is a 7-month window around your 65th birthday.

faqs.question: When can I enroll in Medicare in Alabama?
faqs.answer: Most people first become eligible at age 65. Your Initial Enrollment Period is a 7-month window around your 65th birthday. You can also make changes during the Annual Enrollment Period (October 15 – December 7).

faqs.question: Does Alabama have a Medicare Savings Program?
faqs.answer: Yes. If you have limited income, Alabama Medicaid Agency may help pay your Medicare premiums and cost-sharing through Medicare Savings Programs. Contact your local county office for details.

faqs.question: Where do I report Medicare fraud in Illinois?

faqs.question: When can I enroll in Medicare in Illinois?
faqs.answer: Most people first become eligible at age 65. Your Initial Enrollment Period is a 7-month window around your 65th birthday. You can also make changes during the Annual Enrollment Period (October 15 – December 7).

faqs.question: Does Illinois have a Medicare Savings Program?
faqs.answer: Yes. If you have limited income, Illinois Medicaid (HFS) may help pay your Medicare premiums and cost-sharing through Medicare Savings Programs. Contact your local county office for details.

faqs.answer: Most people first become eligible at age 65. Your Initial Enrollment Period is a 7-month window around your 65th birthday. You can also make changes during the Annual Enrollment Period (October 15 – December 7).

faqs.question: Does Missouri have a Medicare Savings Program?
faqs.answer: Yes. If you have limited income, MO HealthNet may help pay your Medicare premiums and cost-sharing through Medicare Savings Programs. Contact your local county office for details.

faqs.question: When does Medicare open enrollment start in Wyoming?
faqs.answer: Wyoming follows the federal calendar: Annual Enrollment runs October 15 through December 7, the Medicare Advantage Open Enrollment Period runs January 1 through March 31, and your Initial Enrollment is a 7-month window around your 65th birthday.

faqs.question: When can I enroll in Medicare in Wyoming?
faqs.answer: Most people first become eligible at age 65. Your Initial Enrollment Period is a 7-month window around your 65th birthday. You can also make changes during the Annual Enrollment Period (October 15 – December 7).

faqs.question: Does Wyoming have a Medicare Savings Program?
faqs.answer: Yes. If you have limited income, Wyoming Medicaid may help pay your Medicare premiums and cost-sharing through Medicare Savings Programs. Contact your local county office for details.

faqs.answer: The average Medicare Advantage premium in the Dover area is approximately $10/month. Many $0 premium plans are available, though they may have higher copays and narrower networks.

faqs.question: Where can I get free Medicare help in Dover?
faqs.answer: Contact the Delaware Medicare Assistance Bureau (DMAB) at 1-800-336-9500 for free, unbiased Medicare counseling. You can also call 1-800-MEDICARE or visit your local Dover Area Agency on Aging.

stateSpecificOpening: Medicare shoppers in Charleston, WV can get free plan counseling from the West Virginia SHIP at 1-877-987-4463 and review insurer complaint records with the West Virginia Offices of the Insurance Commissioner.
introText: 64 Medicare Advantage, 12 Medigap, and 27 Part D plans are sold in the Charleston, WV area for 2026. Plan networks and drug formularies vary by county, so compare by ZIP code before enrolling.

specialPrograms.description: Low-income residents in Charleston may qualify for help paying Medicare premiums through West Virginia Medicaid (Mountain Health Trust) Medicare Savings Programs.
